configurePresets.name: editor-debug
configurePresets.description: Full development environment with yellowish-purple editor tools.

configurePresets.name: editor-release
configurePresets.description: Optimized build with full editor tools.

configurePresets.name: player-debug
configurePresets.description: Debug player with ImGui debug overlays enabled. Useful for testing.

configurePresets.name: player-profile
configurePresets.description: Optimized build with profiling instrumentation enabled. No editor, no ImGui.

configurePresets.name: player-release
configurePresets.description: Optimized player with no editor/ImGui/profiling. Catalog live-scans the asset dir — fast dev iteration, NOT a shippable artifact. Use ship-release for that.

configurePresets.name: ship-release
configurePresets.description: Shippable artifact: no editor/ImGui/profiling, loads the baked asset_manifest.lua (no FS scan). Canonical desktop/iOS shipping config; Android reaches the same state via the if(ANDROID) block in CMakeLists.txt.

configurePresets.name: ship-mac-universal
configurePresets.description: Shippable macOS .app: universal binary (arm64 + x86_64) so one .dmg runs on both Apple silicon and Intel. Uses the universal-osx overlay triplet so vcpkg deps are fat too. macOS-only — vcpkg's universal triplet drives lipo'd dylibs/static archives that Linux/Windows hosts can't cross-build.

configurePresets.name: ship-ios-device
configurePresets.description: Cross-compile a signed iOS .app for arm64 devices. xcodebuild archives the .app into a .ipa; codesign identity/team set via -DOCTARINE_IOS_DEVELOPMENT_TEAM= and -DOCTARINE_IOS_CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=. Set OCTARINE_PACKAGE_PROJECT to wire the bundle.

configurePresets.name: ship-ios-simulator
configurePresets.description: iOS Simulator build for an Apple-silicon mac. Unsigned. For an Intel host use VCPKG_TARGET_TRIPLET=x64-ios-simulator + CMAKE_OSX_ARCHITECTURES=x86_64.
